agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding by avoiding unfavorable steric clashes in those subtypes.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis is that strong ddG values are sufficient for success, but

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that strong ddG values are sufficient for success, but this iteration shows that selectivity is the limiting factor. The hypothesis is revised to prioritize disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin beyond the current best of 9.1021.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (especially to SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing design on these positions allows us to disrupt conserved pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the FWKT(7-10) pharmacophore and Cys3/Cys14 disulfide. For example, A1K and A2R introduce charged residues that may disrupt off-target hydrophobic interactions.
